current_goal: Lock Midnight Gold Editorial League while preserving the Weekly Poker Clip League product loop.
facts: New reference screenshot has a much stronger premium feel than the previous localhost app.
Colors, typography and restraint are directionally better.
The approved visual direction is Midnight Gold Editorial League.
The homepage must remain product-led with active contest, nominees, votes, time left, rankings, and creator status.
assumptions: Current repo runs locally as documented.
Web MVP is the correct first launch surface.
The immediate business goal is to validate the creator/fan contest loop.
ambiguity_flags: Scheduled finalization and signed uploads remain explicit follow-up decisions.
Legacy App.tsx still contains several page implementations and should continue shrinking.
Premium visuals must not drift into a static VIP-club landing page or gambling-adjacent visual language.

must_check: signed upload or link-first launch decision
maybe_check: security review for local demo credentials

locked_decisions: MVP wedge is Weekly Poker Clip League.
Use existing backend modular monolith; do not create parallel domain models.
Contest/reputation loop is priority over marketplace/AI/mobile.
Adopt Midnight Gold Editorial League as the visual direction.
Use deep midnight/black background, restrained gold accents and editorial serif display typography.
Preserve Weekly Poker Clip League as the product wedge.
recommended_next_action: Continue applying Midnight Gold Editorial League across remaining frontend routes while preserving active contest/voting/ranking content.
fallback_action: If implementation narrows, patch homepage tokens, nav, hero and nominee cards first.
stop_path_signal: Do not claim public beta readiness until upload/link safety and production secret/CORS setup are explicitly accepted.

execution_contract.next_action_instruction: Apply Midnight Gold Editorial League consistently and keep contest mechanics visible.
execution_contract.success_condition_for_next_action: No generic SaaS/debug surfaces remain and homepage shows contest, nominees, votes, rankings, and creator status.
execution_contract.verification_for_next_action: npm run build and focused browser smoke.

causal_bridge: Frontend CI prevents false green before product-facing contest homepage and reputation work.

blocked_by: MVP user journeys not yet proven with real creators/fans

repo_health.structure_fit: Docs live under docs/project-source-of-truth without changing runtime code.
repo_health.ownership_fit: Docs define module ownership and prevent broad template dump.
repo_health.duplication_risk: Low if this folder becomes canonical and older reference material remains reference-only.
repo_health.boundary_violation_risk: Low for docs; medium for upcoming implementation if contest/reputation boundaries are ignored.
repo_health.cleanup_performed: Implemented frontend CI, contest-first homepage, finalization, winner history, creator reputation, analytics v0, founder runbook, design docs, admin contest screen, moderation queue screen, clip reporting, social login, and premium visual direction.
